calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of Mrs. Duvall'S Signed Declaration And Proof Of Enrollment In The Linkage Program
Off Calendar Aug-12-2010 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing To Determine The Results Of Any Further Aps Investigations, Including Whether Or Not Aps Will Refer The Matter To The San Francisco Public Guardian
Off Calendar Jul-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Matter called with case number PTR-05-287904 (Conservatorship of John Duvall)

Appearances: Harry Oliver Gordon for and with John H. Duvall (Conservatee). Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Guardian (SFPG). Marianne Warren from the SFPG.

The Court addressed Mr. Gordon's report. Mr. Gordon stated that he did not have the file with him that contained the signed declaration by Mrs. Duvall. Mr. Gordon stated that he talked to Mrs. Duvall about the treatment of her husband/her leaving the country and noted that Mr. Duvall's Daughter will be the back-up if there is an emergency in the Philippines and Mrs. Duvall needs to leave. Mr. Duvall addressed the Court. Mr. Ferdon stated that they wanted a permanent plan in place, that they had hoped that Counsel would have interviewed a case manager, that they also had hoped that someone other than Karen could come in if there is an emergency, and that they see nothing here today that expands on Mr. Gordon's report. Mr. Gordon stated that the Institute on Aging will charge $200.00 per month and that there will be another program available at a later time with no cost. The Court stated since money is an issue that it didn't want to order the Conservatee to pay for a case manager, that it wants Mr. Oliver to apply for the Institute on Aging's Linkage program and get on their list, and that it wants Mrs. Duvall's signed declaration. The Court also stated that on its own motion it asked for the SFPG to file a petition, excused them of any involvement; thanked them, and stated that if any problems arise there could be an APS referral. Mr. Oliver was ordered to provide Mrs. Duvall's signed declaration and proof of enrollment in the Linkage Program to the Court by 7/22/10. If the documents are not on file by 7/22/10 then Mr. Oliver is to appear on 8/12/10 at 9:30 a.m.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Court Investigator: Molly Glocker. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing To Determine The Results Of Any Further Aps Investigations, Including Whether Or Not Aps Will Refer The Matter To The San Francisco Public Guardian
Off Calendar Jun-03-2010 Continued To Jul-08-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PCN-05-287904 (Conservatorship of John H. Duvall).

Appearances: Gordon Oliver for and with John Duvall. Jonathan Ferdon and Marianne Warren for the San Francisco Public Guardian (SFPG).

The Court stated that it had signed an order on its own motion as to whether or not the SFPG should refile a petition, that the Court continued that matter to 5/6/10, that Mr. Oliver requested a continuance, and that Mr. Ferdon appeared on 5/6/10 and was upset that he didn't receive notice of the continuance. Mr. Oliver stated that his paralegal had stated that notice was given but it wasn't. The Court admonished Mr. Duvall about giving notice in the event of a continuance request. The Court stated that it received the report, that all is well, that there was an unannounced visit, and that the Conservatee was there with his wife. The Court addressed the unsigned, unfiled report by Mr. Oliver. Mr. Oliver stated that again his paralegal didn't file it, then presented an update on his client, and stated that his client's wife is committed to staying and helping. Mr. Duvall addressed the Court. Discussions regarding the non-appearance by Mrs. Duvall, her committment to helping her husband, and the affidavit that she is prepared to sign which is to include a back-up plan for care for her husband, and the possibility of a case manager were held. Mr. Oliver stated that the back-up plan is to use Mr. Duvall's daughter, Karen, if Mrs. Duvall has an emergency in the Philippines. At the conclusion of the discussions, the Court continued both matters to 7/8/10, ordered a declaration re: plans from both Mrs. Duvall and Karen, ordered Mr. Duvall to look into a case manager, and then prepare a declaration outlining in detail his efforts. All declarations are due by close of business on 7/2/10.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Court Investigator: Cynthia Webb-Beckford. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing To Determine The Results Of Any Further Aps Investigations, Including Whether Or Not Aps Will Refer The Matter To The San Francisco Public Guardian
Off Calendar Apr-14-2010 Advanced From May-10-10 To May-06-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing To Determine The Results Of Any Further Aps Investigations, Including Whether Or Not Aps Will Refer The Matter To The San Francisco Public Guardian
Off Calendar Apr-27-2010 Continued To Jun-03-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PTR-05-287904 (Conservatorship of John H. Duvall).

Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Guardian (SFPG).

Mr. Ferdon stated that he had an order from the Court to appear today and presented his copy of the order to the Court for review. The Court stated that the matter had been continued to June 3, 2010. Mr. Ferdon expressed his concern in not being notified that the matter had been continued. The Court apologized and instructed Mr. Ferdon to present his concerns in writing to the Court.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#11312. Court Investigator: Probate Director Mary Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-07-2009 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing To Determine The Results Of Any Further Aps Investigations, Including Whether Or Not Aps Will Refer The Matter To The San Francisco Public Guardian
Off Calendar Dec-07-2009 Continued To May-10-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gordon Oliver for and with John Duvall.

Mr. Oliver stated that Mr. Duvall's wife was sick, that Mr. Duvall was doing ok, referred to the letter he submitted from Mr. Duvall's Doctor, that he had a problem with mobility, that he lived with his Wife and that the Wife was there most of the time. Mr. Duvall stated that he did not remember falling, that he had no problems with his medications, that his Wife helped with buying food and that she no longer had a residence in Daly City. In addition, Mr. duvall stated that the property in the Philippines was vacant, that his Wife was gone the first two months of this year and that he did not understand why his money was tied up. The Court explained that his money was tied up for his protection and that there were grave concerns as to his financial and emotional welfare. The request to release money from the blocked account was denied. The Court will refer the matter to the Public Guardian to determine if a conservatorship is needed. A six month status is set for May 10, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Hopefully Ms. Duvall will be present at that time.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing To Determine The Results Of Any Further Aps Investigations, Including Whether Or Not Aps Will Refer The Matter To The San Francisco Public Guardian
Off Calendar Nov-16-2009 Continued To Dec-07-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Nov-16-2009 Continued To Dec-07-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Authority To Make Withdrawal From Blocked Account Ira Account To Pay Income Taxes Owed By Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-19-2009 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Review
Off Calendar Dec-15-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gordon Oliver for and with Jack Duvall (Trustee). Dwayne Vickery from Adult Protective Services (APS). Susanne Ballam (Daughter) in propria persona. Susan Duvall (Wife) in propria persona.

The Court stated that it had requested a statement of assets, medical report, and a statement from Susan Duvall. Mr. Oliver stated that the documents were attached as exhibits to the declaration. Mr. Vickery presented a brief history to the Court stating that a million dollars was used to purchase property in the Philippines (in Mrs. Duvall and son's name), that Mr. Duvall's bills were unpaid, that Mr. Duvall is left alone a lot, and that he is almost out of assets. The issue of a referral to the Public Guardian was discussed. Ms. Ballam addressed the Court regarding her concerns. Mr. Oliver stated that the proof of assets were in the statement from Franklin Templeton, that the American Fund account had a zero balance, and that he believes Mr. Duvall is the beneficiary on the property in the Philippines. Mr. Duvall stated that he received a letter as proof that he was beneficiary on the property, that he gave the letter to his former attorney, that he can't walk too good but he can still get around, that he drives and shops for himself, that he pays the bills, that he has been to the Philippines five times, and the last time was two years ago. Mrs. Duvall stated that she wants to be with her husband, that she has been depressed about all the bad write-ups about her, that she is on-call/not working right now, and that she just returned from a two month trip to the Phillipines. Mr. Vickery stated that Mrs. Duvall has a separate residence in Daly City. The Court denied the request to release funds from the blocked account noting any further requests must be made by a noticed petition.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Petition For Order Bringing Rev Trust
Off Calendar Jun-22-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Duvall, Potential Conservatee; Nelson Wild, Counsel for the Potential Conservatee; Rena Harel, Petitioner; Nancy Rasch, Counsel for the Petitioner.

These matters were heard together: Case #287904 and #287906.

Mr. Wild and Ms. Rasch agreed to a stipulation that Mr. Duvall would be restored as trustee of The John Duvall Revocable Trust under Court supervision without accounting. An Order would be prepared by Mr. Wild stating that Mr. Duvall would be restored as trustee of the Trust on condition that 2 IRA Accounts: Franklin and Schwab, would be put into blocked accounts and that no access, aside from income distribution would be made without a court order.

The Petition for Conservatorship of the Estate was denied. Ms. Harel would submit Final Accounting as Temporary Conservator two weeks before August 14, 2006. Bond was discharged and accounting was waived for Ms. Harel.

A review date was set for November 17, 2008. At that time, four matters must be addressed: 1)Mr. Duvall must make a statement showing proof of his assets, supported by bank statements 2) Mr. Duvall must discuss his living situation 3)A medical report must be submitted to show that he is thriving 4)Mrs. Duvall must submit a statement regarding how she is providing care for Mr. Duvall or how she is securing care for him.

Receipts for blocked accounts are due two weeks before July 17, 2006.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Reporter: Helen Soret-Matley CSR 225; Court Investigator: Melinda Gean; Bailiff: Deputy Mizota; Clerk: Candace Lum.

Petition For Order Bringing Rev Trust
Off Calendar May-25-2006 Continued To Jun-22-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rena Harel (Temporary Conservator); John Duvall (Conservatee); Nelson Wild (Counsel for the Conservatee); Nancy Rasch (Counsel for the Temporary Conservator); Rodel Rodis (Counsel for Susan Duvall, wife of the Conservatee, who was present in court but did not appear).

After extensive presentation by Mr. Wild in opposition to the conservatorship and rebuttal by Ms. Rasch, the Court found that there was insufficient evidence to support the continuation of the Temporary Conservatorship. However, due to certain concerns presented by Ms. Rasch, the Court continued the hearing on the general conservatorship Petition to June 22, 2006 to allow the parties sufficient time to address issues concerning the preservation and retention of assets in the United States via the Trust. As such, the Court extended the tenure of Ms. Harel as temporary successor trustee to the continued hearing date of June 22, 2006. Ms. Rasch is to prepare the order.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy McMath; Reporter: Janet Pond CSR# 5292; Court Investigator: Shauna Gillespie-Ford; Bailiff: Deputy Mizota; Clerk: R. Michael Diles.

Petition For Order Bringing Rev Trust
Off Calendar Mar-23-2006 Continued To May-25-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch (Counsel for Petitioner). No other appearances were made.

Ms. Rasch appeared for the purpose of confirming the continuance of this matter to May 25, 2006 and presenting an Order extending the appointment of the Temporary Trustee to the same date (which Order was signed by the Commissioner in open court).

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Reporter: Ron St. Clair CSR# 1170; Court Investigator: Melinda Gean; Bailiff: None; Clerk: R. Michael Diles.
